title: The right DVD release
wikitext:
[]: The "right" DVD release?             Quote Post by Yue » Mon Jul 18, 2011 2:47 pm It was my birthday last week, so as a present to myself I'm gonna stop waiting for a blu-ray release and get Evangelion on DVD. I don't think I'm ready to have EoE in the house yet, but the proper series...yeah. I don't know which version I should be looking for though. The platinum collection, the perfect collection, the holiday platinum collection, the perfect platinum collection...@_@ Also I heard one of them comes with a NERV parking pass sticker for your car. That sounds kind of badass, but I can't find which one that is, or if it's the "correct" DVD release.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 18, 2011 2:55 pm There's a 6-disc thinpack collection Eva Monkey likes because the episodes themselves are larger video files. I have the Perfect:Platinum Collection, which comes in a metal tin case. I think the Christmas box set has the exact same discs for a bit cheaper. It has all of the episodes in a 7-disc set including really cool extras like the original story-board/animatic reels to a few of the episodes, but also has some weird extras like a video of the English dub director's wife and her giant Asuka collection. And I don't know why...

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Mon Jul 18, 2011 2:58 pm Really you can't go wrong with any of the platinum releases. I don't know about the older ones but whatever the hell the latest release is, (I think it's the one with Shinji holding Rei on the cover) it's pretty good and reasonably priced. FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: but also has some weird extras like a video of the English dub director's wife and her giant Asuka collection. And I don't know why...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jul 18, 2011 3:43 pm imma just leave this here: http://svenge.pcriot.com/evasite/usplat/usplat.htm I prefer this one: http://svenge.pcriot.com/evasite/usplat/platthin.htm It's like a superbit release. No useless audio/video extras hogging precious bits. The booklets are transcripted on my site, the rest of the extras have been put on Youtube, and most of the audio commentaries are the sort of thing you will listen to ONCE and then never again. My review: http://www.evamonkey.com/reviews/platinum-thinpak.php

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 18, 2011 9:29 pm I don't always have a good internet connection, so I prefer to have the animatics on hard disc. That, and I found the metal tin edition for all of $30, which I found later to be unreasonably inexpensive. Tarnsman wrote: Because for some unholy reason they think you/we care?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jul 18, 2011 11:09 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Yeah, I honestly felt ripped off at that point. The video is, in my opinion, evidence that Matt Greenfield and the rest of ADV are insane.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jul 19, 2011 3:25 am @Yue: Based on the assumption that you are in the United States, I did some price shopping for you, and here's what I came up with: Platinum Complete (6 DVDs, no extras, higher bitrate) $38.86 + shipping @ Walmart http://www.walmart.com/ip/4242492 Platinum Holiday Edition (7 DVDs, on-disc extras, lower bitrate) $39.99 + shipping @ Right Stuf http://www.rightstuf.com/cgi-bin/catalogmgr/browse/item/81583/4/0/0 BTW, the one with the NERV parking pass sticker was the 1st pressing of the original Platinum: 01 single release, which is long out-of-print.

[]:             Quote Post by Yue » Tue Jul 19, 2011 3:26 am svenge wrote: BTW, the one with the NERV parking pass sticker was the 1st pressing of the original Platinum: 01 single release, which is long out-of-print.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jul 19, 2011 3:34 am Yue wrote: Curses, foiled again!

[]:             Quote Post by Yue » Tue Jul 19, 2011 4:32 am svenge wrote: Well, I found an auction (ending Tuesday evening) that has the original Platinum DVD singles and the seller's picture shows that it comes with the NERV sticker. You could always bid on that... (NOTE: I have no financial interest in the aforementioned auction)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 19, 2011 6:50 am svenge wrote: Well, I found an auction (ending Tuesday evening) that has the original Platinum DVD singles and the seller's picture shows that it comes with the NERV sticker. You could always bid on that... (NOTE: I have no financial interest in the aforementioned auction)

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jul 19, 2011 9:15 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Now, this is coming from someone who thinks dubbing is an idiotic waste of time at worst, and a shameful marketing tactic at best. So I find 90% of what's said in dub commentaries to be completely worthless. The animatics are kind of interesting, but only to a very narrow group of people.

[]:             Quote Post by Yue » Tue Jul 19, 2011 3:54 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Has anyone else observed that the parking pass can melt easily?

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jul 19, 2011 9:17 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Haha! True. I'm reminded of Tiffany and Allison's commentary of Episode 8 and feeling how much time I wanted back after viewing it. I guess I would be among that narrow group of people who are interested in the animatics enough to buy the Perfect Platinum. In fact, those are the only extras I really like on the discs in the first place. The rest I just find to be crap.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 19, 2011 9:24 pm Yue wrote: I've asked a friend that has it; she's noticed no such thing.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jul 19, 2011 9:28 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: A friend of mine put it on his back window, and I swear it looked like someone put a blow torch to it, and this is just from a Maryland summer.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jul 20, 2011 7:44 am svenge wrote: The interesting thing about the animatics is that ADV carried over all but one of the AR Rush Videos (aka Animatics) from the Renewal Monolith box set bonus disc. They got all 7 TV episodes (9, 15, 18, 19, 20, 23, and 24) and the selection from "Air", but not the selection from "Rebirth".

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Wed Jul 20, 2011 7:50 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: They probably either couldn't get the rights to it (which would be odd considering they got everything else), or they didn't have enough space on the discs after putting their own commentaries, the video of Greenfield's wife and collection, and the "Mythology of Evangelion" videos on it (which, even if it isn't true, would sadly make more sense to me). Or they were planning on putting it in the extras of Death &amp Rebirth or The End of Evangelion before loosing the right to it to Manga Entertainment. Though, I'm not sure how all that went down.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jul 20, 2011 8:23 am svenge wrote: There would've been room on Vol. 7 since the "missing" Rebirth animatic is about 8 minutes long and the disc has only 6.5GB of data.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Wed Jul 20, 2011 8:26 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: But, isn't their "Mythology" video about 8 minutes long? And their audio commentary probably wasn't helping matters either. Silly ADV.... I didn't want to hear your thoughts on the show. There are plenty of reviewers from outside resources for that. I wanted to see the show and the elements from the show's making itself.

I don't think I'm ready to have EoE in the house yet, but the proper series...yeah. I don't know which version I should be looking for though.
